question.text: A designated person of a listed company bought 1,000 shares on March 1 and sold them on July 15 of the same year. What is the compliance status of this transaction?

answer.text: It is a contra trade violation as it was executed within six months
It is compliant as more than four months have elapsed
It is compliant as it was done during the trading window
It requires only pre-clearance, not contra trade compliance

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Schedule B, clause 9, designated persons shall not execute a contra trade within six months. March 1 to July 15 is approximately 4.5 months, which is less than six months, making this a violation. **The Trap:** Candidates may calculate the period incorrectly or assume that passing the quarter-end is sufficient. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Profits from such contra trades must be disgorged for remittance to SEBI's Investor Protection and Education Fund.
